item.title: Welcome to Trevor's Notes
item.description: What is Trevor’s Notes? My name is Trevor Kafka, I am a math and physics tutor, and Trevor’s Notes is a project that began in July 2025.

item.title: Combinations and Permutations
item.description: Permutations A permutation is an ordered arrangement of distinct items.

item.title: Probability through Counting
item.description: Lots of problems in probability are really problems in creative counting.

item.title: Vectors
item.description: Problems titled “Giancoli” are sourced from Physics: Principles with Applications by Giancoli.

item.title: Circles
item.description: A circle is a set of points in a plane that are at a fixed distance away from a chosen point, called the center of the circle.

item.title: Conic Sections
item.description: This page is under construction and is far from complete.

item.title: Greek letters
item.description: Letters in the Greek alphabet are used sometimes in mathematics and physics in addition to the familiar Latin alphabet (Aa, Bb, Cc, etc.) to provide additional symbols to help make it easier to distinguish different concepts.

item.title: Sectors
item.description: A sector is a pie-shaped segment of a circle formed by connecting a circle’s center with line segments to two points on the circle itself, as shown in the diagram below.

item.title: Volume
item.description: Prisms The volume of a prism can be found by multiplying the base area of the prism A by the height of the prism h.
